grammar_points.name: 基本问候语 (Basic Greetings)
grammar_points.explanation: 你好 (nǐ hǎo) is the most common greeting in Chinese. It can be used at any time of day.

examples.chinese: 你好！", "pinyin": "Nǐ hǎo!", "english": "Hello!
您好！", "pinyin": "Nín hǎo!", "english": "Hello (polite)!

grammar_points.name: 礼貌用语 (Polite Expressions)
grammar_points.explanation: 谢谢 (xièxie) expresses gratitude. 不客气 (bú kèqi) is the standard response. 对不起 (duìbuqǐ) is used to apologize, and 没关系 (méi guānxi) means 'it's okay'.

examples.chinese: 谢谢！", "pinyin": "Xièxie!", "english": "Thank you!
不客气！", "pinyin": "Bú kèqi!", "english": "You're welcome!
对不起！", "pinyin": "Duìbuqǐ!", "english": "Sorry!
没关系！", "pinyin": "Méi guānxi!", "english": "It's okay!

grammar_points.name: 感谢与回应 (Expressing Thanks and Responses)
grammar_points.explanation: 谢谢你 (xièxie nǐ) or just 谢谢 expresses thanks. Common responses include 不客气 (bú kèqi), 不谢 (bú xiè), or 没关系 (méi guānxi).

examples.chinese: 谢谢你！", "pinyin": "Xièxie nǐ!", "english": "Thank you!
不客气！", "pinyin": "Bú kèqi!", "english": "You're welcome!

grammar_points.name: 告别 (Farewells)
grammar_points.explanation: 再见 (zàijiàn) is the standard way to say goodbye.

examples.chinese: 再见！", "pinyin": "Zàijiàn!", "english": "Goodbye!

grammar_points.name: 疑问代词"什么" (Interrogative Pronoun 什么)
grammar_points.explanation: 疑问代词"什么"表示疑问，用在疑问句中可直接做宾语，或者与后接名词性成分一起做宾语。The interrogative pronoun 什么 is used in interrogative sentences, serving as the object by itself or together with a nominal element following it.

examples.chinese: 你叫什么名字？", "pinyin": "Nǐ jiào shénme míngzi?", "english": "What's your name?
这是什么？", "pinyin": "Zhè shì shénme?", "english": "What is this?
这是什么书？", "pinyin": "Zhè shì shénme shū?", "english": "What book is this?

grammar_points.name: "是"字句 (The 是 Sentence)
grammar_points.explanation: "是"字句是由"是"构成的判断句，用于表达人或事物等于什么或者属于什么。其否定形式是在"是"前加上否定副词"不"。A 是 sentence is a determinative sentence with 是, indicating what somebody or something equals or belongs to. The negative is formed by adding 不 before 是.

examples.chinese: 李月是老师。", "pinyin": "Lǐ Yuè shì lǎoshī.", "english": "Li Yue is a teacher.
我是美国人。", "pinyin": "Wǒ shì Měiguó rén.", "english": "I am American.
我不是老师。", "pinyin": "Wǒ bú shì lǎoshī.", "english": "I am not a teacher.

grammar_points.name: 用"吗"的疑问句 (Interrogative Sentences with 吗)
grammar_points.explanation: 疑问助词"吗"表示疑问语气，用在陈述句句尾构成疑问句。The particle 吗 indicates an interrogative mood. When added at the end of a declarative sentence, it turns the statement into a question.

examples.chinese: 你是美国人吗？", "pinyin": "Nǐ shì Měiguó rén ma?", "english": "Are you American?
你是中国人吗？", "pinyin": "Nǐ shì Zhōngguó rén ma?", "english": "Are you Chinese?
你是老师吗？", "pinyin": "Nǐ shì lǎoshī ma?", "english": "Are you a teacher?

grammar_points.name: 疑问代词"谁" (Interrogative Pronoun 谁)
grammar_points.explanation: 谁 (shéi/shuí) is used to ask about people. It can be the subject or object in a sentence.

examples.chinese: 她是谁？", "pinyin": "Tā shì shéi?", "english": "Who is she?
你是谁？", "pinyin": "Nǐ shì shéi?", "english": "Who are you?

grammar_points.name: 疑问代词"哪" (Interrogative Pronoun 哪)
grammar_points.explanation: 哪 (nǎ) is used to ask about choices or options. It must be followed by a measure word and noun.

examples.chinese: 你是哪国人？", "pinyin": "Nǐ shì nǎ guó rén?", "english": "Which country are you from?
你是哪个班的？", "pinyin": "Nǐ shì nǎ ge bān de?", "english": "Which class are you in?

grammar_points.name: 结构助词"的" (Structural Particle 的)
grammar_points.explanation: 的 is used to show possession or attribution. It connects a modifier to a noun.

examples.chinese: 我的朋友", "pinyin": "wǒ de péngyou", "english": "my friend
她的书", "pinyin": "tā de shū", "english": "her book
汉语老师", "pinyin": "Hànyǔ lǎoshī", "english": "Chinese teacher

grammar_points.name: 动词"有" (Verb 有)
grammar_points.explanation: 有 (yǒu) means 'to have' or 'there is/are'. The negative is 没有 (méiyǒu), not 不有.

examples.chinese: 我家有三口人。", "pinyin": "Wǒ jiā yǒu sān kǒu rén.", "english": "My family has three people.
我没有弟弟。", "pinyin": "Wǒ méiyǒu dìdi.", "english": "I don't have a younger brother.
教室里有学生。", "pinyin": "Jiàoshì li yǒu xuésheng.", "english": "There are students in the classroom.

grammar_points.name: 量词 (Measure Words)
grammar_points.explanation: Chinese uses measure words between numbers and nouns. Different nouns require different measure words. 口 (kǒu) is used for family members, 个 (gè) is the most common general measure word.

examples.chinese: 三口人", "pinyin": "sān kǒu rén", "english": "three family members
一个朋友", "pinyin": "yí ge péngyou", "english": "one friend
两个女儿", "pinyin": "liǎng ge nǚ'ér", "english": "two daughters

grammar_points.name: 助词"了" (Particle 了)
grammar_points.explanation: 了 indicates a change of state or completed action. After numbers, it shows the number has been reached.

examples.chinese: 我女儿今年八岁了。", "pinyin": "Wǒ nǚ'ér jīnnián bā suì le.", "english": "My daughter is eight years old this year.
我有两本书了。", "pinyin": "Wǒ yǒu liǎng běn shū le.", "english": "I now have two books.

grammar_points.name: 能愿动词"会" (Modal Verb 会)
grammar_points.explanation: 会 (huì) expresses ability or skill learned through practice. It indicates knowing how to do something.

examples.chinese: 我会说汉语。", "pinyin": "Wǒ huì shuō Hànyǔ.", "english": "I can speak Chinese.
你会做中国菜吗？", "pinyin": "Nǐ huì zuò Zhōngguó cài ma?", "english": "Can you cook Chinese food?
我不会游泳。", "pinyin": "Wǒ bú huì yóuyǒng.", "english": "I can't swim.

grammar_points.name: 形容词谓语句 (Adjectival Predicates)
grammar_points.explanation: In Chinese, adjectives can function as predicates without a verb. 很 (hěn) is often added for rhythm, not necessarily emphasis.

examples.chinese: 我的汉语不好。", "pinyin": "Wǒ de Hànyǔ bù hǎo.", "english": "My Chinese is not good.
她很漂亮。", "pinyin": "Tā hěn piàoliang.", "english": "She is very pretty.
今天很冷。", "pinyin": "Jīntiān hěn lěng.", "english": "Today is very cold.

grammar_points.name: 日期表达 (Date Expressions)
grammar_points.explanation: Chinese dates are expressed from largest to smallest unit: year, month, day. Use 号 (hào) or 日 (rì) for days in spoken and written Chinese respectively.

examples.chinese: 二零二六年四月三日", "pinyin": "Èr líng èr liù nián sì yuè sān hào", "english": "April 3, 2026
今天几号？", "pinyin": "Jīntiān jǐ hào?", "english": "What's the date today?
三月八号", "pinyin": "Sān yuè bā hào", "english": "March 8th

grammar_points.name: 星期表达 (Days of the Week)
grammar_points.explanation: Use 星期 (xīngqī) or 周 (zhōu) followed by numbers 1-6 for Monday through Saturday. Sunday is 星期日/天 or 周日/天.

examples.chinese: 星期一", "pinyin": "xīngqī yī", "english": "Monday
星期日", "pinyin": "xīngqī rì", "english": "Sunday
今天星期几？", "pinyin": "Jīntiān xīngqī jǐ?", "english": "What day is it today?

grammar_points.name: 能愿动词"想" (Modal Verb 想)
grammar_points.explanation: 想 (xiǎng) expresses desire or intention to do something. It's softer than 要 (yào).

examples.chinese: 我想买苹果。", "pinyin": "Wǒ xiǎng mǎi píngguǒ.", "english": "I want to buy apples.
你想吃什么？", "pinyin": "Nǐ xiǎng chī shénme?", "english": "What do you want to eat?
我想去中国。", "pinyin": "Wǒ xiǎng qù Zhōngguó.", "english": "I want to go to China.

grammar_points.name: 钱的表达 (Money Expressions)
grammar_points.explanation: Chinese uses 块 (kuài) for yuan in spoken Chinese. 元 (yuán) is the formal written form. 多少钱 (duōshao qián) asks about price.

examples.chinese: 这个多少钱？", "pinyin": "Zhège duōshao qián?", "english": "How much is this?
五块钱。", "pinyin": "Wǔ kuài qián.", "english": "Five yuan.
太贵了！", "pinyin": "Tài guì le!", "english": "Too expensive!

grammar_points.name: 能愿动词"要" (Modal Verb 要)
grammar_points.explanation: 要 (yào) expresses strong desire or intention. It's more direct than 想.

examples.chinese: 我要这个。", "pinyin": "Wǒ yào zhège.", "english": "I want this one.
你要去哪儿？", "pinyin": "Nǐ yào qù nǎr?", "english": "Where are you going?
我要一杯咖啡。", "pinyin": "Wǒ yào yì bēi kāfēi.", "english": "I would like a cup of coffee.

grammar_points.name: 助动词"可以" (Modal Verb 可以)
grammar_points.explanation: 可以 (kěyǐ) expresses permission or possibility. It asks if something is allowed or possible.

examples.chinese: 我可以进来吗？", "pinyin": "Wǒ kěyǐ jìnlái ma?", "english": "May I come in?
这里可以停车。", "pinyin": "Zhèli kěyǐ tíngchē.", "english": "You can park here.

grammar_points.name: 能愿动词"会"表示能力 (Modal Verb 会 for Ability)
grammar_points.explanation: 会 (huì) indicates learned ability or skill. Unlike 能 (néng) which indicates physical possibility, 会 means you have learned how to do something.

examples.chinese: 你会说英语吗？", "pinyin": "Nǐ huì shuō Yīngyǔ ma?", "english": "Can you speak English?
我会写汉字。", "pinyin": "Wǒ huì xiě Hànzì.", "english": "I can write Chinese characters.
他不会开车。", "pinyin": "Tā bú huì kāichē.", "english": "He can't drive.

grammar_points.name: 语言名称 (Language Names)
grammar_points.explanation: Language names in Chinese are formed by adding 语 (yǔ) or 文 (wén) to country names.

examples.chinese: 汉语/中文", "pinyin": "Hànyǔ/Zhōngwén", "english": "Chinese
英语", "pinyin": "Yīngyǔ", "english": "English
日语", "pinyin": "Rìyǔ", "english": "Japanese

grammar_points.name: 动作动词 (Action Verbs)
grammar_points.explanation: Action verbs like 喝 (hē - drink), 吃 (chī - eat), 看 (kàn - see/read) describe actions.

examples.chinese: 我喝茶。", "pinyin": "Wǒ hē chá.", "english": "I drink tea.
我吃米饭。", "pinyin": "Wǒ chī mǐfàn.", "english": "I eat rice.
我看书。", "pinyin": "Wǒ kàn shū.", "english": "I read books.

grammar_points.name: 表达喜好 (Expressing Preferences)
grammar_points.explanation: 喜欢 (xǐhuan) expresses liking something. 不喜欢 (bù xǐhuan) is the negative.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢喝茶。", "pinyin": "Wǒ xǐhuan hē chá.", "english": "I like drinking tea.
我不喜欢吃苹果。", "pinyin": "Wǒ bù xǐhuan chī píngguǒ.", "english": "I don't like eating apples.

grammar_points.name: 疑问代词"怎么" (Interrogative Pronoun 怎么)
grammar_points.explanation: 怎么 (zěnme) asks about manner or method. It's used to ask 'how' something is done.

examples.chinese: 你怎么去学校？", "pinyin": "Nǐ zěnme qù xuéxiào?", "english": "How do you go to school?
这个字怎么读？", "pinyin": "Zhège zì zěnme dú?", "english": "How do you read this character?
怎么说？", "pinyin": "Zěnme shuō?", "english": "How do you say it?

grammar_points.name: 交通方式 (Transportation)
grammar_points.explanation: Use 坐 (zuò) or 骑 (qí) to express mode of transportation. 坐 is for vehicles you sit in, 骑 is for bikes/motorcycles.

examples.chinese: 我坐公共汽车去学校。", "pinyin": "Wǒ zuò gōnggòng qìchē qù xuéxiào.", "english": "I take the bus to school.
我骑车去。", "pinyin": "Wǒ qíchē qù.", "english": "I ride a bike there.
我走路去。", "pinyin": "Wǒ zǒulù qù.", "english": "I walk there.

grammar_points.name: 介词"在"表示地点 (Preposition 在 for Location)
grammar_points.explanation: 在 (zài) indicates location where an action takes place. It comes before the verb in Chinese.

examples.chinese: 我在宿舍学习。", "pinyin": "Wǒ zài sùshè xuéxí.", "english": "I study in the dormitory.
他在图书馆看书。", "pinyin": "Tā zài túshūguǎn kàn shū.", "english": "He reads in the library.
我们在餐厅吃饭。", "pinyin": "Wǒmen zài cāntīng chīfàn.", "english": "We eat in the cafeteria.

grammar_points.name: 住表示居住 (Verb 住 for Living)
grammar_points.explanation: 住 (zhù) means to live or reside somewhere.

examples.chinese: 我住在北京。", "pinyin": "Wǒ zhù zài Běijīng.", "english": "I live in Beijing.
你住在哪儿？", "pinyin": "Nǐ zhù zài nǎr?", "english": "Where do you live?

grammar_points.name: 正反疑问句 (Affirmative-Negative Questions)
grammar_points.explanation: To form a yes/no question, you can repeat the verb in positive and negative form. This is called a V-not-V question.

examples.chinese: 你认识不认识他？", "pinyin": "Nǐ rènshi bu rènshi tā?", "english": "Do you know him?
你去不去？", "pinyin": "Nǐ qù bu qù?", "english": "Are you going?
他是不是老师？", "pinyin": "Tā shì bu shì lǎoshī?", "english": "Is he a teacher?

grammar_points.name: 认识 vs 知道 (To Know a Person vs To Know a Fact)
grammar_points.explanation: 认识 (rènshi) means to know or be acquainted with a person. 知道 (zhīdao) means to know a fact or information.

examples.chinese: 我认识他。", "pinyin": "Wǒ rènshi tā.", "english": "I know him (as a person).
我知道他的名字。", "pinyin": "Wǒ zhīdao tā de míngzi.", "english": "I know his name (as a fact).

grammar_points.name: 介词"给" (Preposition 给)
grammar_points.explanation: 给 (gěi) introduces the beneficiary or recipient of an action. It can mean 'for' or 'to'.

examples.chinese: 我给你介绍一下。", "pinyin": "Wǒ gěi nǐ jièshào yīxià.", "english": "Let me introduce you.
我给你买一本书。", "pinyin": "Wǒ gěi nǐ mǎi yì běn shū.", "english": "I'll buy a book for you.
我给妈妈打电话。", "pinyin": "Wǒ gěi māma dǎ diànhuà.", "english": "I'll call my mother.

grammar_points.name: 动词重叠 (Verb Reduplication)
grammar_points.explanation: Single-syllable verbs can be reduplicated to indicate a brief, casual action. Adding 一 (yī) makes it more natural: V一V.

examples.chinese: 介绍一下", "pinyin": "jièshào yīxià", "english": "briefly introduce
看一下", "pinyin": "kàn yīxià", "english": "take a quick look
想一想", "pinyin": "xiǎng yīxiǎng", "english": "think about it briefly
